objective: Audit premium model policy, banned-model enforcement, Depth Guard, bounded spawning, partial/failure handling, stop, teardown, and recovery behavior.
summary_findings: Worker leaf prompts are present, but leaf enforcement is still policy-driven because bash remains available and queue-writing recursion is not explicitly blocked in the worker agent.
Commander guidance documents max depth, max workers, and premium model policy, but runtime enforcement for depth exhaustion, retry/timeout budgets, and squad-lead anti-recursion is incomplete.
Skill-level metaswarm launch instructions describe SS-250 shape but do not hard-cap commander count or explicitly block nested metaswarm recursion; sealed-test fan-out also lacks a spawn budget.
Depth-guard lint covers core worker invariants, but misses delegation phrase checks, recursive commander/squad-lead checks, and stronger launch-proof validation.
Launcher teardown/recovery is incomplete for metaswarm children: only pane PIDs and run-level state are updated, so commander progress files and nested child agents can survive stop/teardown paths.
consensus_notes: Consensus: worker leaf lock text exists and premium model pool/banned-model policy are documented.
Consensus: most gaps are enforcement gaps rather than missing documentation—especially for squad-lead recursion, launch budgets, and teardown/recovery telemetry.
Consensus: stop/recovery handling is weaker than launch-path documentation.
conflict_notes: No material conflicts across worker findings; worker 5 primarily narrowed failure-mode gaps to launcher teardown and queue sizing.
